title: Episode 33: Hand Edition — Vol. 1, Issue 5
description: Today's hand edition explores cutting-edge approaches to wrist and hand surgery, from novel biomechanical insights to innovative treatment strategies. We examine whether ulnar shortening alone can restore DRUJ stability, compare radiolucent and titanium plating materials, analyze the understudied opposite dart-throwing motion, evaluate virtual reality surgical training, identify CT-based risk factors for complications, and assess arthroscopic treatment of post-traumatic stiffness.

title: Episode 32: Foot & Ankle Edition — Vol. 1, Issue 5
description: Today's foot and ankle edition examines six key areas of clinical practice: surgical timing considerations in ankle fracture fixation, advanced imaging techniques for hindfoot alignment assessment, radiation safety in minimally invasive procedures, three-dimensional aspects of hallux valgus pathology, morphological risk factors for flatfoot deformity, and evidence-based perioperative medication management.

title: Episode 31: Shoulder & Elbow Edition — Vol. 1, Issue 5
description: Today's shoulder and elbow edition examines six key studies spanning surgical timing, risk assessment, navigation technology, repair techniques, clinical decision-making, and imaging comparisons. We explore whether timing affects distal humerus hemiarthroplasty outcomes, compare metabolic markers to frailty indices for predicting complications, evaluate navigation benefits in reverse shoulder arthroplasty, assess muscle advancement techniques across tear patterns, investigate surgeon decision-making between arthroplasty options, and validate MRI measurements against CT for instability assessment.

title: Episode 30: Knee Edition — Vol. 1, Issue 5
description: Today's knee edition covers six open-access studies spanning surgical anatomy, joint preservation, pediatric sports medicine, pain prediction, anesthesia techniques, and thromboembolism prevention. Key findings include anatomical variations in distal femur morphology for TKA planning, promising results for proximal fibular osteotomy in Western populations, excellent outcomes for combined ACL-ALL reconstruction in skeletally immature patients, development of chronic pain prediction models, equivalence of surgical versus ultrasound-guided adductor canal blocks, and evidence against routine thromboprophylaxis after ACL surgery.

title: Episode 29: Hip Edition — Vol. 1, Issue 5
description: Today's hip edition covers six open-access studies spanning the full spectrum of hip care, from innovative palliative approaches to complex trauma management. We examine a novel intrathecal pain block for frail elderly patients with hip fractures, challenge assumptions about MRI necessity in osteonecrosis treatment, highlight critical gaps in osteoporosis care, identify what really matters for survival after intertrochanteric fractures, demonstrate how hip replacement reduces fall risk, and explore acetabular fracture patterns in resource-limited settings.

title: Episode 28: Sports Medicine Edition — Vol. 1, Issue 4
description: Today's sports medicine edition examines six key areas of current research: rotator cuff repair techniques with and without subscapularis involvement, surgical positioning strategies for shoulder stabilization, reverse shoulder arthroplasty design evolution, evidence-based treatment of first-time shoulder dislocations, innovative approaches to osteochondral lesion management, and the critical role of psychological readiness in adolescent athletes returning to sport after shoulder surgery.

title: Episode 27: Trauma Edition — Vol. 1, Issue 4
description: This trauma edition covers six key studies spanning fracture management techniques and outcomes. Topics include optimal tibial entry portals for intramedullary nailing, staged conversion strategies after external fixation, three-dimensional printed implants in polytrauma reconstruction, ulnar shortening procedures, mandibular fracture complications, and advances in bone cyst treatment approaches.

title: Episode 26: Hand Edition — Vol. 1, Issue 4
description: Today's hand edition covers six diverse topics in upper extremity care, from rare causes of carpal tunnel syndrome to healthcare economics and alternative treatments. We examine tophaceous gout as an uncommon but important cause of median nerve compression, review outcomes from the largest series of complex perilunate fracture-dislocations, explore traditional Ayurvedic thermal therapy for trigger thumb, discuss advanced three-dimensional reconstruction techniques, analyze healthcare costs for vulnerable worker populations, and evaluate ulnar shortening osteotomy for combined pathology.

title: Episode 25: Foot & Ankle Edition — Vol. 1, Issue 4
description: Today's foot and ankle edition covers six key studies examining surgical timing, innovative fixation techniques, and outcome predictors. We explore how surgical timing affects wound complications in ankle fractures, review a novel posterior plating technique for challenging periprosthetic fractures, compare fusion versus ORIF for neuropathic ankle fractures, investigate vitamin D's role in hallux valgus surgery outcomes, identify the side hop test as a key return-to-sport predictor, and examine combined osteotomy techniques for complex tibiocalcaneal reconstruction.

title: Episode 24: Shoulder & Elbow Edition — Vol. 1, Issue 4
description: Today's shoulder and elbow edition covers six compelling studies examining surgical timing, risk assessment, navigation technology, repair techniques, salvage procedures, and clinical decision-making. We explore whether acute versus salvage distal humerus hemiarthroplasty affects outcomes, compare metabolic assessment to frailty evaluation for predicting complications, evaluate navigation benefits in reverse shoulder arthroplasty, assess muscle advancement techniques across tear patterns, review innovative reconstruction for recurrent instability, and examine factors influencing surgeon implant selection.

title: Episode 23: Knee Edition — Vol. 1, Issue 4
description: Today's knee edition covers six important studies spanning the full spectrum of knee surgery. We examine how different weight loss interventions affect outcomes before joint replacement, compare PROMIS instruments to traditional outcome measures, analyze muscle recovery patterns after knee arthroplasty, evaluate fast-track protocols across hospital types, investigate hamstring graft diameter effects in ACL reconstruction, and compare meniscal root repair techniques.

title: Episode 22: Hip Edition — Vol. 1, Issue 4
description: Today's hip edition examines six critical studies spanning complications, interventions, and techniques in hip arthroplasty. We explore early intraprosthetic dislocation mechanisms in dual-mobility systems, unusual trochanteric bursa pathology, cognitive behavioral approaches to post-surgical kinesiophobia, comparative effectiveness of regional anesthesia techniques, traction table methods for dislocation reduction, and bacterial colonization patterns of retained hardware during revision surgery.

title: Episode 21: Sports Medicine Edition — Vol. 1, Issue 3
description: Today's sports medicine edition examines six key areas of athletic orthopedic care. We explore cost-effectiveness comparisons between shoulder instability procedures, assess the overlooked issue of graft site morbidity in ACL reconstruction, and review innovative rotator cuff repair techniques. The episode also covers practical considerations for corticosteroid injection timing, presents an integrated approach to patellar instability management, and analyzes return-to-sport outcomes following triceps tendon repairs in athletes.

title: Episode 20: Trauma Edition — Vol. 1, Issue 3
description: Today's trauma edition covers six diverse studies examining biomarkers for nonunion prediction, contemporary approaches to femoral neck fractures, rehabilitation integration in trauma systems, innovative nerve reconstruction techniques, surgical decision-making patterns, and challenging traditional assumptions about scaphoid malunion outcomes.

title: Episode 19: Hand Edition — Vol. 1, Issue 3
description: Today's Hand Edition examines six key studies spanning fracture care innovation, surgical risk assessment, treatment durability, and healthcare disparities. We explore fluoroscopy-free techniques for distal radius fractures in resource-limited settings, compare surgical risks between liver disease severities, analyze long-term recurrence rates after Dupuytren contracture treatments, evaluate comorbidity scoring systems, discuss disparities in congenital hand surgery, and investigate glycemic control's impact on trigger finger outcomes.

title: Episode 18: Foot & Ankle Edition — Vol. 1, Issue 3
description: Today's foot and ankle edition covers six diverse topics spanning reconstruction techniques, rehabilitation innovations, diagnostic validation, perioperative care, and surgical timing considerations. We examine a comprehensive approach to late compartment syndrome reconstruction, evaluate digital rehabilitation programs for Achilles repairs, validate portable force sense testing devices, review anesthetic considerations for pregnant patients, analyze surgical timing effects on wound complications, and explore arthroscopic techniques for talar neck fractures.

title: Episode 17: Shoulder & Elbow Edition — Vol. 1, Issue 3
description: Today's shoulder and elbow edition covers six open-access studies examining both established and emerging treatment approaches. We explore long-term functional outcomes after reverse shoulder arthroplasty using advanced motion analysis, examine comprehensive biologic augmentation protocols for massive rotator cuff repairs, and analyze pediatric elbow fracture patterns that may be easily missed. Additionally, we review complex elbow trauma management, innovative anesthetic techniques for olecranon surgery, and comparative outcomes of distal humerus hemiarthroplasty timing.

title: Episode 16: Knee Edition — Vol. 1, Issue 3
description: Today's knee edition covers six diverse topics spanning surgical outcomes, regional anesthesia, diagnostic tools, and complications. We examine survival rates for hybrid unicompartmental knee arthroplasty, compare isolated versus combined approaches for patellar instability, evaluate liposomal bupivacaine effectiveness in regional blocks, validate Chinese pain assessment tools, present a rare fungal infection case, and challenge assumptions about component rotation significance.

title: Episode 15: Hip Edition — Vol. 1, Issue 3
description: Today's hip edition covers six open-access studies examining revision acetabular reconstruction with highly porous mega cups, the role of nutritional status in predicting chronic pain after hip fracture, liver function markers as mortality predictors, bone turnover biomarkers in chronic kidney disease patients, validation of digital mobility outcomes, and management strategies for complex multi-level femoral fractures.

title: Episode 14: Sports Medicine Edition — Vol. 1, Issue 2
description: Today's sports medicine edition covers six open-access studies examining innovative approaches to musculoskeletal conditions. We explore epidural adhesiolysis for chronic radicular pain, exosome therapy for knee osteoarthritis, advanced hydrogel systems for meniscus repair, copper-ion releasing bone substitutes, multifidus muscle atrophy as a predictor of fusion complications, and the unexpected anti-inflammatory properties of paroxetine in bone disease.

title: Episode 13: Trauma Edition — Vol. 1, Issue 2
description: Today's trauma edition examines six diverse studies spanning surgical techniques, drug delivery innovations, and implant complications. We explore a novel curved guide system for vertebroplasty that combines single-puncture simplicity with bilateral-quality cement distribution, achieving superior pain relief compared to standard unilateral approaches. The episode covers emerging extracellular vesicle platforms for drug delivery, offering biological alternatives to synthetic carriers with enhanced biocompatibility and targeting capabilities. We discuss groundbreaking research on resveratrol's targeting of iron metabolism pathways in disc degeneration, revealing ferritinophagy as a novel therapeutic target. Additionally, we review creatine supplementation's expanding evidence base beyond traditional performance enhancement, including applications for injury recovery and tactical populations. The program examines immune microenvironment profiling in angiosarcoma, where specific macrophage populations predict clinical outcomes and suggest targeted therapeutic approaches. Finally, we analyze mechanism failures in motorized lengthening nails, revealing material-specific failure patterns that inform device selection and patient counseling strategies.

title: Episode 12: Hand Edition — Vol. 1, Issue 2
description: Today's hand edition covers six diverse topics from chronic infections to metabolic regulation. We explore a case of twelve-year retained wooden foreign body causing osteomyelitis, analyze patterns in negative online reviews of hand surgeons, discuss unexpected clear cell sarcoma discovery during carpal tunnel release, examine delayed extensor tendon rupture after ulnar plating, evaluate fibular graft augmentation for proximal humerus fractures, and review emerging evidence on osteocyte regulation of adipose tissue browning.

title: Episode 11: Foot & Ankle Edition — Vol. 1, Issue 2
description: Today's foot and ankle edition covers six diverse studies spanning surgical techniques, conservative management, and emerging pathology. We review biomechanical evidence favoring semitendinosus over Achilles allografts for hamstring reconstruction, discuss complex polydactyly management strategies, examine evidence-based acupuncture guidelines for diabetic foot care, evaluate a novel hook plate system for calcaneal fractures, highlight an emerging fungal pathogen, and assess growth factor therapy for chronic heel tendinopathies.

title: Episode 10: Shoulder & Elbow Edition — Vol. 1, Issue 2
description: Today's shoulder and elbow edition examines six key studies: outcomes of RASL reconstruction for scapholunate injuries showing excellent long-term function, AI-powered diagnosis of SLAP lesions with promising accuracy, reduced rotator cuff re-tear rates in patients taking GLP-1 receptor agonists, comparative outcomes of soft tissue reconstructions for irreparable tears favoring pectoralis minor and latissimus dorsi transfers, successful circular external fixation for complex gunshot fractures in resource-limited settings, and comprehensive outcomes of Achilles tendon allografts in upper extremity reconstruction.

title: Episode 9: Knee Edition — Vol. 1, Issue 2
description: Today's knee edition covers six open-access studies examining fracture morphology in flexion varus tibial plateau injuries, anatomical variability in distal femur morphology, perioperative corticosteroid protocols, cruciate ligament degeneration mechanisms, bicondylar tibia fracture fixation techniques, and conservative ACL management approaches using biological augmentation.

title: Episode 8: Hip Edition — Vol. 1, Issue 2
description: Today's hip edition covers six open-access studies spanning gap estimation accuracy in knee arthroplasty, long-term outcomes of hip fracture treatments, emerging three-dimensional printing applications, complex pediatric hip dysplasia management, post-traumatic arthritis reconstruction techniques, and innovative hip fracture risk prediction modeling. These studies provide actionable insights for improving surgical precision, treatment selection, and patient risk stratification in hip and related orthopaedic care.

title: Episode 7: Sports Medicine Edition — Vol. 1, Issue 1
description: In this episode of OrthoDigest, we cover six open-access sports medicine studies examining whether adding a tibial tubercle osteotomy to MPFL reconstruction increases morbidity, how surgeon bias and athletic level influence clavicle fracture treatment decisions, whether a normal MRA should preclude surgery for clinical posterior shoulder instability, return-to-sport outcomes after distal triceps tendon rupture repair in athletes, early clinical results of arthroscopic distal clavicle autograft for glenoid bone loss, and sex-specific longitudinal hamstring development in adolescent alpine skiers with implications for ACL injury prevention."Equivalent outcomes without increased morbidity in MPFL reconstruction and distalising tibial tubercle osteotomy versus isolated MPFL reconstruction" — Lun DKY et al., J Orthop — https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jor.2026.03.011"The role of patient and surgeon characteristics on the treatment decision for displaced midshaft clavicle fractures in athletes: a global survey" — Plug LGE et al., JSES Rev Rep Tech — https://doi.org/10.1016/j.xrrt.2025.100662"No difference in outcomes for posterior shoulder instability surgery in patients with a normal vs. pathological radiologist reported magnetic resonance arthrogram study" — Byrd JA et al., JSES Rev Rep Tech — https://doi.org/10.1016/j.xrrt.2026.100675"Distal triceps tendon rupture repair results in high return to sport rates for amateur and professional athletes: a systematic review" — Vandenberg CJ et al., JSES Rev Rep Tech — https://doi.org/10.1016/j.xrrt.2026.100694"Arthroscopic distal clavicle autograft with suture button fixation for shoulder instability with glenoid bone loss: a case series" — Ruderman BF et al., JSES Rev Rep Tech — https://doi.org/10.1016/j.xrrt.2026.100667"Longitudinal changes in hamstring muscle morphology in adolescent competitive alpine skiers are sex-specific: a 4-year cohort study" — Fitze DP et al., Ann Med — https://doi.org/10.1080/07853890.2026.2620897

title: Episode 6: Trauma Edition — Vol. 1, Issue 1
description: In this episode of OrthoDigest, we cover six open-access trauma studies examining a novel trans-symphyseal curved intramedullary device for anterior pelvic ring fractures, the impact of surgical timing on wound complications after ankle fracture fixation over a twenty-two-year dataset, a meta-analysis of three-dimensional printing and virtual planning in periarticular fracture surgery, circular external fixation as definitive single-stage treatment for gunshot humeral fractures in rural Kenya, the superiority of preoperative metabolic and nutritional indices over frailty scores for predicting complications after proximal humerus fracture surgery, and a large meta-analysis supporting reverse total shoulder arthroplasty over hemiarthroplasty and open reduction and internal fixation for geriatric proximal humerus fractures."Trans-symphyseal fixation for complex anterior pelvic fractures: a novel technique guide and case series" — Quercetti NF et al., OTA Int — https://doi.org/10.1097/OI9.0000000000000479"Impact of surgical timing on wound complications following ankle fracture fixation: A 22-year retrospective study" — Zahra et al., J Clin Orthop Trauma — https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jcot.2026.103405"Impact of 3D technology on perioperative outcomes of articular and periarticular fractures: A systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials" — Mazzola et al., J Orthop — https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jor.2026.03.015"High-velocity gunshot-related humeral fractures managed with circular external fixation in a resource-limited setting" — Sciuto SA et al., OTA Int — https://doi.org/10.1097/OI9.0000000000000476"Does pre-operative metabolic and nutritional assessment have greater association with perioperative outcomes than frailty following surgical management of proximal humerus fractures?" — Williamson et al., JSES Rev Rep Tech — https://doi.org/10.1016/j.xrrt.2026.100686"Outcomes of reverse total shoulder arthroplasty vs. other surgical fixation methods for proximal humerus fractures in the geriatric population: A systematic review and meta-analysis" — Mekhail et al., JSES Rev Rep Tech — https://doi.org/10.1016/j.xrrt.2025.100644

title: Episode 5: Hand Edition — Vol. 1, Issue 1
description: In today's Hand edition, we compare recurrence rates of Dupuytren contracture after limited fasciectomy, needle aponeurotomy, and collagenase injection, examine whether early NSAID prescriptions increase scaphoid nonunion risk, and evaluate ultrasound-guided versus endoscopic carpal tunnel release in a multicenter matched cohort. We also review flexor tenolysis outcomes after zone 2-3 flexor tendon repairs, describe a concurrent bilateral arthroscopic approach to proximal pole scaphoid nonunion, and assess whether the Charlson Comorbidity Index and Charlson Fracture Index predict complications after distal radius fracture fixation."Likelihood of Dupuytren Contracture Recurrence After Limited Fasciectomy, Needle Aponeurotomy or Collagenase: Systematic Review of Prospective Data With 2- to 7-Year Follow-up" — Nocek M et al., Journal of Hand Surgery Global Online — https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jhsg.2026.100979"Early Nonsteroidal Anti-Inflammatory Drug Prescriptions and Nonunion After Scaphoid Fractures: A TriNetX Matched Cohort Study" — Phan A et al., Journal of Hand Surgery Global Online — https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jhsg.2026.100958"Prospective Multicenter Propensity Score-matched Comparison of Ultrasound-guided Versus Endoscopic Carpal Tunnel Release" — Marwin VM et al., Journal of Hand Surgery Global Online — https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jhsg.2026.100974"Clinical Outcomes of Flexor Tenolysis Following Zone 2-3 Flexor Tendon Repair: A Retrospective Review" — Shigley C et al., Journal of Hand Surgery Global Online — https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jhsg.2025.100941"Arthroscopic Bone Grafting in Bilateral Proximal Pole Scaphoid Fracture Nonunion: An Illustrated Description" — Ganesan DN et al., Journal of Wrist Surgery — https://doi.org/10.1055/s-0044-1796672"Association of Charlson Comorbidity Index and Charlson Fracture Index With Surgical Complications in Distal Radius Fractures: A Retrospective Review" — Parker AM et al., Journal of Hand Surgery Global Online — https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jhsg.2026.100959

title: Episode 4: Foot & Ankle Edition — Vol. 1, Issue 1
description: In today's Foot & Ankle edition, we examine whether the timing of ankle fracture fixation affects wound complication rates, compare open Broström, arthroscopic Broström, and lasso-loop techniques for lateral ankle instability, and evaluate the impact of 3D printing technology on articular fracture surgery outcomes. We also review subtalar arthroereisis implant removal rates in adult flatfoot correction, explore a novel non-opioid analgesic for post-bunionectomy pain, and compare two cases of peroneal tendon pathology to illustrate why anatomical findings must be interpreted in clinical context."Impact of surgical timing on wound complications following ankle fracture fixation: A 22-year retrospective cohort study" — Zahra W et al., Journal of Clinical Orthopaedics and Trauma — https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jcot.2026.103405"Meta-analysis of surgical approaches to lateral ankle instability: Open Broström versus arthroscopic Broström versus lasso-loop repair" — Cho T et al., Journal of Orthopaedics — https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jor.2026.02.014"Impact of 3D technology on perioperative outcomes of articular and periarticular fractures: A systematic review and meta-analysis of RCT studies" — Mazzola MA et al., Journal of Orthopaedics — https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jor.2026.03.015"Removal rates of subtalar arthroereisis implants in adult flexible planovalgus correction: A case series" — Carter D et al., Journal of Orthopaedics — https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jor.2026.02.028"Pregabalin naproxencarbil in acute post-bunionectomy pain: a randomized clinical trial" — Jiang GL et al., PAIN Reports — https://doi.org/10.1097/PR9.0000000000001424"Peroneus brevis tenosynovitis associated with hypertrophied peroneal tubercle: Comparative analysis of 2 cases and their etiologies" — Michailidis A et al., Radiology Case Reports — https://doi.org/10.1016/j.radcr.2026.02.001

title: Episode 3: Shoulder & Elbow Edition — Vol. 1, Issue 1
description: In today's Shoulder & Elbow edition, we examine whether intraoperative navigation improves outcomes in reverse total shoulder arthroplasty, explore the protective role of bisphosphonates in osteoporotic shoulder arthroplasty patients, and evaluate a novel metabolic-nutritional index for predicting complications after proximal humerus fracture surgery. We also cover the muscle advancement technique in combined rotator cuff and subscapularis tears, compare hyaluronic acid formulations for adhesive capsulitis, and ask whether distal humerus hemiarthroplasty outcomes differ between acute and salvage settings."The role of intraoperative navigation in reverse total shoulder arthroplasty and its impact on clinical outcomes" — Brian A et al., JSES Reviews, Reports & Techniques — https://doi.org/10.1016/j.xrrt.2026.100687"Shoulder arthroplasty in the osteoporotic patient: do bisphosphonates make a difference?" — Adams NC et al., JSES International — https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jseint.2026.101629"Does pre-operative metabolic and nutritional assessment have greater association with perioperative complications than frailty?" — Williamson TK et al., JSES Reviews, Reports & Techniques — https://doi.org/10.1016/j.xrrt.2026.100686"Clinical outcomes of muscle advancement technique for arthroscopic rotator cuff repair: comparison between cases with and without subscapularis tears" — Okubo A et al., JSES International — https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jseint.2026.101633"Intra-articular injection of high versus low molecular weight hyaluronic acid in adhesive capsulitis; randomized trial" — Mir M et al., Future Science OA — https://doi.org/10.1080/20565623.2025.2581460"Functional outcomes and complications of distal humerus hemiarthroplasty performed in acute versus salvage cases" — Gosselin C et al., JSES International — https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jseint.2026.101639

title: Episode 2: Knee Edition — Vol. 1, Issue 1
description: In this knee edition, we review six open-access studies covering alignment philosophy and strength recovery in total knee arthroplasty, surgical decision-making for periprosthetic distal femur fractures, adductor canal block protocols for postoperative pain, the neuroscience of proprioceptive loss after ACL injury, and an in vitro strategy to prevent biofilm on polypropylene mesh used in revision knee surgery."Restricted kinematic alignment in total knee arthroplasty achieves equivalent or superior functional outcomes to mechanical alignment without compromising implant survivorship: A systematic review" — Merchant et al., J Orthop — https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jor.2026.02.025"Temporal changes in quadriceps and hamstring strength and flexibility following total knee arthroplasty" — Shetty et al., J Orthop — https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jor.2026.03.008"Open reduction and internal fixation versus distal femoral replacement for periprosthetic distal femur fractures: a systematic review and meta-analysis" — Brady et al., OTA Int — https://doi.org/10.1097/OI9.0000000000000475"No difference in pain control and hospitalisation length between single-shot adductor canal block and continuous adductor canal block in total knee arthroplasty: A systematic review and meta-analysis" — Migliorini et al., J Orthop — https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jor.2026.02.024"Disrupted sensorimotor control after ACL injury: from mechanoreceptor degeneration to neuroplasticity-oriented rehabilitation" — Lee et al., Ann Med — https://doi.org/10.1080/07853890.2025.2604403"Pretreatment With Vancomycin Prevents Staphylococcal Biofilm Formation on Marlex Mesh" — Chao et al., Arthroplast Today — https://doi.org/10.1016/j.artd.2026.101985

title: Episode 1: Hip Edition — Vol. 1, Issue 1
description: In this inaugural episode of OrthoDigest, we cover six open-access hip surgery studies examining the hemiarthroplasty versus internal fixation debate for unstable intertrochanteric fractures, dual mobility cup safety in elderly THA, the link between femoroacetabular impingement morphology and silent osteoarthritis in over-sixties, an umbrella meta-analysis of delirium risk factors after hip arthroplasty, predictors of contralateral secondary hip fractures from a large Japanese prospective cohort, and the neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio as a novel refracture risk biomarker."Hemiarthroplasty for unstable intertrochanteric hip fractures: A systematic review and meta-analysis" — Tang Z et al., J Orthop — https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jor.2026.01.019"Safety of posterolateral approach with a polished cemented stem and a modular dual mobility implant in patients over 75 years old" — Meschini C et al., J Orthop — https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jor.2026.02.042"Femoroacetabular impingement–related deformities correlate with advanced osteoarthritis in asymptomatic caucasians over 60: A CT-based cross-sectional study" — Blum SFU et al., J Orthop — https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jor.2026.02.041"Risk factors for delirium after hip arthroplasty: An umbrella meta-analysis" — Jiang X et al., Int J Nurs Stud Adv — https://doi.org/10.1016/j.ijnsa.2026.100494"Identification of factors predictive of contralateral secondary hip fractures in patients after initial hip fracture - A prospective observational study" — Takata S et al., Bone Rep — https://doi.org/10.1016/j.bonr.2026.101908"Preoperative neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio and refracture risk in hip fracture patients: a propensity score-matched analysis" — La R et al., Ann Med — https://doi.org/10.1080/07853890.2026.2615552
